title: [Bug]: Group natural-language messages silently dropped in 2026.4.11 (regression of #29637/#30591)
body:
### Summary

In 2026.4.11, group natural-language messages are silently dropped between gateway intake and agent invocation. Slash commands (e.g. `/whoami`) in the same group do get processed and return a reply. DMs to the same bot work normally.

### Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.4.11 (`769908e`)
- Telegram channel, long-polling
- Bot privacy mode: disabled
- Bot status in group: `member`
- Group is a regular group

### Observed behavior

1. `/whoami` in the group works.
2. Natural-language text in the same group gets no reply.
3. `lastUpdateId` advances, so the gateway consumed the update.
4. `sessions.json` is updated, but no JSONL file is created.
5. No errors or skip reasons are logged.

### Reproduction

1. Fresh OpenClaw 2026.4.11 install
2. Configure Telegram channel for the group
3. Disable bot privacy mode
4. Add bot to a regular group
5. Send `/whoami` → reply appears
6. Send `hello` → silent drop

### Workaround in use

Both users DM the bot separately. That works, but loses the shared group conversation.
